card_description: Parmenides (Greek: "Son of Parmenes") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Parmenides is pre-socratic philosopher of unchanging being. PKD engages with the Parmenidean One as counterpoint to Heraclitean flux. Appears in 16 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Parmenides. Context: "Dick details the transition from the "Father" stage (1974 - Justice/Warrior) to the "Son" stage (1975 - Spring King/Dancing Youth)."
